And then lastly, here's number six in the system. I want you to be rewarding your customers and thanking them for their support. Now if your company doesn't have some type of referral program already in place, ask them about starting one or ask your manager if it's okay to incentivize people yourself directly if they refer customers to you. It can be the difference between success and failure in your business, especially when it comes to dealing with professionals like lawyers and accountants and financial plans where they expect to get something in return and often rightfully so if it's customary and legal in their industry. And I mentioned legal only because there are certain industries like the legal industry where referral fees are heavily regulated and can be considered illegal kickbacks have done the wrong way. So again, just be careful and check with your manager to find exactly what your boundaries are and then operate within them. Now that said, very often the most powerful thing you can do is to simply send a sincere thank you note to let your client know how much it means to you to have their trust and support a handwritten sincere thank you note trumps everything except if you have a really, really no money hungry professional and you got to do that as well. So I want you to be sure to use these six simple steps to get as many referrals as you possibly can because referrals can and should be the lifeblood of your business. They're the best way to ensure that you never have to wonder where your next piece of major business is coming from in the future. So in closing, if I were to sum up what the system is really all about and it's most fundamental level, I would say there's really one simple concept that everything else is built on and it's called preparedness. Have you ever read the book or heard about the book Sun Tzu, the Art of War? Well, one of the basic tenants in that book is that every battle is one before it's ever fought. Well, that's exactly what the straight line system is all about. It's about being perfectly prepared for every possible eventuality and knowing exactly what you're going to do next. It's about knowing your scripts and your pattern, about identifying needs, qualifying the right way by asking the exact right questions from big picture down to the specifics, right? If there's pain, you dig it out and amplify if necessary and you keep looping around and building the prospect up on the three tens as you ask for the order calmly and elegantly and of course without pressure, ten, ten, ten and you keep asking for that order and you will close everybody who's closeable that way. And the most important thing to remember with the system is that good enough factor. See, even if you just implement a few of the distinctions of the straight line system, you will see your closing rate ramp up dramatically. So take the next 60 days as you're learning this and stretch yourself. You know, work harder, make more calls, go out there and really give it your all. And once you've mastered the systems from a part of you, the true beauty of it is that it will translate to virtually every aspect of your life. And of course, you can only use this system ethically for the right reasons. See, it's an extremely powerful system. I've said this many times, I want to say it again. It's extremely important. You don't use it to get people to do things that they should not do, to buy things they should not buy. This is about ethical persuasion, which means influencing people to do the things that they should be doing to make their lives better. Anything else is simply not acceptable. But when this system is used to influence the right people, you will not only dramatically increase your sales results, but you'll make a huge difference in the quality of their lives. You can help people move past the barriers and the obstacles that have stopped them from getting what they want in life in the past. And that's my goal for you as well, to use the skills that I'm giving you here so you can get what you want out of life and not be a creature of circumstance, but be a creator of circumstance. All the success you've ever dreamed of is just waiting for you to reach out and grab it. So it's up to you to take the next step. I look forward to hearing your major stories of success.
